title: New Feature: Introducing the Audit Log in Tracket
excerpt: For anyone who needs a clean audit trail, the new Audit Log in Tracket lets you trace activity end-to-end. You can see who did what and when, from time entry events (created, updated, deleted) to the full timesheet fl…

posts.author: jaimy (Jaimy @Tracket)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday Community _ audit log2465×784 92.6 KB

For anyone who needs a clean audit trail, the new Audit Log in Tracket lets you trace activity end-to-end. You can see who did what and when, from time entry events (created, updated, deleted) to the full timesheet flow (submitted, approved, rejected, or reopened).

When something looks off, or it’s time for an audit, you can open an event to see the full details—what changed (including old vs. new values on updates) and any rejection message when a timesheet is rejected.

This makes it much easier to support compliance checks and resolve discrepancies quickly.

Read more on the Audit Log on our website.
